coreIssue: This junior Brand Designer performance conversation is about making the quality bar concrete enough for Carlos to apply feedback in his day-to-day work.
openingQuestion: What does strong work for a junior Brand Designer look like to you right now, and which recent piece best matches that bar?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent project or deliverable instead of speaking only in generalities
whether if he describes prior feedback, he can point to a concrete change in his process, decisions, or revisions
whether he hesitates or gets vague when asked what feels hardest to improve right now
avoid: do not turn the conversation into your verdict on taste without showing concrete examples tied to junior-level expectations
do not stack multiple improvement areas at once before you have tested how he currently defines good work
goodOutcome: You and Carlos have agreed what good junior Brand Designer work looks like in one or two concrete examples and one immediate practice he will use in upcoming design work.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick two recent pieces of Carlos's work—one closer to the bar and one not yet there—so you can compare them against explicit junior-level expectations.
